ddls:
CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the researcher */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the researcher */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the researcher */,
  "nation" TEXT /* Nation of the researcher */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the researcher */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the researcher */,
  "field_research" TEXT /* Field of research of the researcher */,
  "country_work" TEXT /* Country where the researcher works */,
  "phd_year" INTEGER /* Year the researcher completed their PhD */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution where the researcher is affiliated */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the researcher */,
  "research_interests" TEXT /* Detailed description of the researcher's research interests */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the researcher */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the researcher's profile was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the researcher's profile was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "participant_groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the participant group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the participant group */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the participant group was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the participant group was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researcher_participant_groups" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher */,
  "group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id", "group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researcher_experience" (
  "experience_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experience */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher */,
  "experience_years" INTEGER /* Number of years of experience */,
  "experience_type" TEXT /* Type of experience (e.g., academic, industry) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution where the experience was gained */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the experience */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the experience */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the experience */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the experience record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the experience record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experience_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_researcher_experience_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the researcher's information */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the researcher's information was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the access log was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)
